description: Rustynet MCP server manifest — four stdio JSON-RPC servers providing repo context, gate running, lab state management, and a configurable AI sub-agent for AI agents.

servers.name: rustynet-mcp-repo-context
servers.description: Structured access to the Rustynet documentation graph, requirements, security controls/findings, architecture constraints, role transitions, platform support, and crate structure. which_crate gives a file's layer + boundary rule; get_crate_dependencies gives a crate's forward + reverse deps (blast radius before patching a shared crate). 17 tools + MCP resources (docs) + prompts.
servers.resources: Curated documentation index exposed as rustynet-doc:// resources (resources/list + resources/read).
servers.working_dir: <baked at build time via build.rs; CWD-independent>

servers.name: rustynet-mcp-gate-runner
servers.description: Runs Rustynet quality gates on demand — cargo fmt, check, clippy, test, build, audit, deny, and CI gate scripts. run_security_gates bundles the local security-critical suite (secrets hygiene, backend-boundary leakage, dataplane-leak, security-regression, supply-chain, role-auth, secret-redaction, traversal-adversarial, active-network) + audit/deny into one pass/fail verdict — the 'did I break security?' check. list_gate_scripts is categorized (security / role / phase / release / lab); run_gate_scripts runs any chosen set. Every command runs under a kill-on-timeout watchdog. 11 tools.
servers.working_dir: <baked at build time via build.rs; CWD-independent>

servers.name: rustynet-mcp-lab-state
servers.description: Queries/manages the UTM VM lab AND drives the autonomous overnight live-lab loop (run → catch bugs → patch → re-verify) via async background jobs. Live-lab runs launch detached (start_live_lab_run returns a job_id instantly, survives an MCP-server reload); poll get_job_status (completion-record-first, pid-reuse-safe), read structured results with get_run_result, read logs with bounded tail_job_log / read_report_artifact, search them with grep_report, jump to a stage's evidence with get_stage_log, reclaim disk with prune_jobs. Track convergence across iterations with get_run_trend (GREEN / STUCK / MOVING verdict). Runs default to source-mode working-tree (deploys uncommitted patches), auto-topology (fills Windows+macOS from the inventory for full 3-OS coverage), and accept a >24h timeout_secs. Fast re-verify after a per-node code patch: rebuild_nodes redeploys only the affected node (others keep state) + skip_soak — no full multi-node rebuild. (No mid-test-stage resume exists; setup-stage failures can resume_from=prepare_source_archive. explain_stage spells out the exact re-verify command.) wait_for_job blocks until done (no busy-poll); explain_stage maps a failed stage to its owning file + common causes; get_lab_topology gives a secret-free node/role digest. Full VM power control (power_on_vm / power_off_vm[force] / get_vm_power_state), reachability triage (check_vm_reachable = power-state + TCP probe), out-of-band Linux-guest diagnostics + network reset for unreachable guests (get_vm_network_info / reset_vm_network via utmctl exec, no SSH), and host_disk_status let an agent recover the lab and manage disk over a long run with no human. preflight_check is a one-call loop-start go/no-go (host tools + ssh material + inventory + disk + deploy set + every node's power/TCP). what_will_deploy previews exactly what a run ships to the VMs for a source_mode (tracked in, untracked out) so a patch is never tested stale. diff_runs compares two runs' stage outcomes (helped vs regressed). write_loop_note / get_loop_journal are a durable append-only journal under state/ that survives context compaction, so the agent remembers what it already tried across a 24h+ run. get_run_progress gives live mid-run visibility (elapsed, last-activity age with a hang flag, latest log lines, artifacts so far) between wait_for_job calls. find_untested_work aggregates the run-matrix history into a prioritized work queue (regressed / never-passed / never-run / stale-green) so the agent is handed a target instead of hunting. seed_cargo_cache re-syncs each guest's OFFLINE cargo registry to the workspace Cargo.lock after a lock change (tar+scp only the missing .crate blobs + sparse-index entries into the guest registry root, Unix + Windows/PowerShell) so `cargo build --offline` stops failing with `no matching package`. Short ops run under a kill-on-timeout watchdog. NETWORK-PROFILE CONTRACT (LiveLabVmConnectivityRulebook): run tools are VERIFY-ONLY — audit_lab_network is the read-only fleet attachment/drift audit, preflight_check(profile)/ensure_lab_ready(profile) re-verify a profile and return the evidence path + digest, start_live_lab_run(network_profile) records the profile immutably and enforces an explicit id fail-closed, and a live reverse-SOCKS bootstrap tunnel BLOCKS evidence launches. The ONLY mutation path is prepare_lab_network with explicit approve_reconfigure (atomic lease + stopped-VM apply + verified rollback; restore_lab_network undoes it); apply_vm_bridged_network is DEPRECATED and always refuses (the AppleScript/en0 path was removed; en0 is denied by policy); recovery tools (reset_vm_network / apply_host_route_fix) invalidate prior network evidence and force a fresh preflight. 51 tools + an overnight-loop prompt.
servers.working_dir: <baked at build time via build.rs; CWD-independent>

notes: The repo root is baked into each binary at compile time (build.rs), so the servers resolve documents/ and crates/ regardless of the client's working directory. Override with the RUSTYNET_REPO_ROOT env var if needed. rustynet-mcp-ai-agent's ai_agent tool reads the LOCAL repo (read-only) and resolves the baked repo root like the other servers; its three proxy tools (ai_read/write/read_write) do not need it.

ai_agent_server.name: rustynet-mcp-ai-agent
ai_agent_server.description: Calls a configured LLM provider as a first-class sub-agent and exposes standardized live-lab loop helpers. Five built-in, zero-registry-file providers — deepseek (default), grok (xAI), kimi (Moonshot), glm (Zhipu), qwen (Alibaba DashScope) — plus any other OpenAI-Chat-Completions-compatible API via an optional registry (see llm_provider below). Tools: prompt proxies (ai_read/write/read_write) whose `model` parameter accepts 'flash'/'pro' shortcuts OR any literal model id unchanged, ai_list_models (READ-ONLY: fetches the active provider's LIVE model list via its OpenAI-compatible models endpoint — not hardcoded, so a caller can discover and use models the two shortcuts don't cover), ai_check_balance (READ-ONLY: the active provider's account balance/credit, when it has a balance_url configured — confirmed live for deepseek; not every provider exposes one), ai_agent (READ-ONLY grounded repo+UTM lab researcher), ai_lab_run (deterministic live-lab launch + triage only on fail unless triage_on_failure=false), ai_next_live_lab_target (read-only run-matrix target chooser), ai_autonomous_live_lab_loop (reconcile stale jobs → choose next target → launch), ai_recover_lab_environment (async stop-after-ready recovery after interruption), ai_reconcile_jobs (repair stale singleton-blocking labrun records), ai_live_lab/ai_live_lab_result (failure triage + async polling), and ai_doc_sync (read-only docs edit proposal). Each provider's key resolves from its own env var (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY/GROK_API_KEY/KIMI_API_KEY/GLM_API_KEY/QWEN_API_KEY, or a registry entry's api_key_env); DeepSeek additionally falls back to ~/Desktop/deepseek_api.md or ~/.deepseek_api_key for backward compatibility.

llm_provider.description: The 'flash'/'pro' model tiers, API endpoint, and balance-check endpoint used by rustynet-mcp-ai-agent are resolved from an LlmProvider, not hardcoded. Five built-in presets (deepseek/grok/kimi/glm/qwen — see built_in_provider in ai_agent.rs) each work out of the box with just RUSTYNET_LLM_PROVIDER=<name> + their own *_API_KEY env var, no registry file needed. Optional non-secret registry file (default ~/.config/rustynet/llm_providers.json, override path via RUSTYNET_LLM_PROVIDERS_FILE) adds any OTHER OpenAI-Chat-Completions-compatible provider (Groq, Together, Fireworks, OpenAI, a local Ollama shim, ...), or overrides one of the five built-in presets, with no code change; switch the active one via RUSTYNET_LLM_PROVIDER=<name> without editing the file. The registry never stores a key value, only the name of the env var holding it (api_key_env, default {NAME}_API_KEY). The model list itself is NOT hardcoded either: ai_list_models fetches it live from the active provider's models_url (an OpenAI-compatible GET endpoint, derived from base_url by convention if not set explicitly — swap /chat/completions for /models), and any ai_* tool's `model` argument accepts any id from that list directly, not just the 'flash'/'pro' shortcuts. balance_url has no derivation convention (unlike models_url) — only deepseek's is confirmed live; add one to a registry entry once you've found the right endpoint for another provider.

grok.balance_check: not configured — no confirmed endpoint yet

kimi.balance_check: not configured — no confirmed endpoint yet

glm.balance_check: not configured — no confirmed endpoint yet

qwen.balance_check: not configured — no confirmed endpoint yet

llm_provider.example_note: models_url is optional in a real entry — omit it and it derives from base_url by convention; shown explicitly here only to document the field. This example adds a SIXTH provider (groq) beyond the five built-ins; the registry is only needed to go beyond those five or to override one of their presets.
